meta.title: Quantum Machine KnitWeb Seed Index
meta.note: Seed catalogue for the public GUI. Live device availability, calibration, and commercial access change often; each record should keep source URLs and provenance.
meta.record_policy: Local additions are stored in browser localStorage and can be exported as KnitWeb machine records.

wnw_intel.name: WNW Quantum Systems Intel
wnw_intel.positioning: A narrow-web / between-worlds index for specifying quantum-machine facts before expanding into broad web or LLM retrieval.
wnw_intel.query_policy: Specify intent, machine class, access path, and proof budget first; estimate rows and proof size before fetching or knitting larger sub-weaves.

layers.name: Machine identity warp
layers.purpose: Stable machine IDs, provider IDs, aliases, source URLs, and provenance.
layers.small_device_budget: tiny; enough for offline dropdowns and exact record exchange
layers.quantum_mapping: One warp per quantum machine or processor family.

layers.name: Coordinal positioning
layers.purpose: Place machines in architecture, modality, status, access, geography, and time dimensions.
layers.small_device_budget: small; filter dimensions fit in a compact edge cache
layers.quantum_mapping: Traverse the catalogue as a 360-degree coordinate field instead of a linear list.

layers.name: Evidence weft
layers.purpose: Weave source-backed claims across machines, vendors, papers, and cloud access routes.
layers.quantum_mapping: Each claim about qubits, topology, benchmark, or availability is a provenance-bearing pick.

layers.name: Query-size estimator
layers.purpose: Estimate candidate rows, relations, and proof bytes before full retrieval.
layers.small_device_budget: tiny compute; avoids wasteful broad crawling
layers.quantum_mapping: Useful for small-device p2p requests and AR/VR previews.

layers.name: KnitWeb record export
layers.purpose: Export the selected machine as a machine record with graph edges and source provenance.
layers.quantum_mapping: Turns machine details into portable narrow-web facts.

layers.name: Broad-web fallback
layers.purpose: Escalate to search, vendor docs, or LLM summarization only when the specified narrow query is underspecified.
layers.quantum_mapping: Keeps the seed index honest while allowing new machines to be discovered.

machines.name: IBM Quantum System Two / Heron
machines.qubits: three Heron processors in System Two; 133 physical qubits per 2023 Heron generation

details.processor: Fixed-frequency superconducting qubits with tunable couplers.
details.connectivity: Heavy-hex style IBM topology; modular System Two integration.
details.benchmark: IBM described Heron as a 3-5x device-performance improvement over Eagle in 2023.
details.software: Qiskit Runtime, OpenQASM, IBM Quantum Platform

machines.caveats: Exact public backend names and qubit counts can change by plan and generation.

machines.name: IBM Condor
machines.access: not generally listed as a public cloud backend

details.processor: Large superconducting processor based on IBM cross-resonance gate technology.
details.connectivity: Designed as a scale and yield milestone.
details.benchmark: IBM positioned Condor as comparable in performance to Osprey while breaking 1000 qubits.

machines.caveats: Scale milestone, not a general-purpose public service record.

machines.name: IBM Osprey
machines.access: IBM Quantum ecosystem where exposed historically

details.processor: 433-qubit superconducting processor.
details.benchmark: More than tripled the 127-qubit Eagle processor count.

machines.caveats: Historical milestone; not necessarily a currently available backend.

machines.name: IBM Eagle
machines.access: IBM Quantum ecosystem where exposed historically

details.processor: 127-qubit superconducting processor generation preceding Osprey and Heron.
details.benchmark: Often used as a baseline against newer Heron results.

machines.caveats: Availability should be checked against the live IBM platform.

machines.name: IBM Hummingbird

details.processor: Superconducting generation before Eagle.
details.benchmark: A major IBM scale step before the 100+ qubit era.

machines.caveats: Public backend availability is historical.

machines.name: IBM Falcon

details.processor: Superconducting transmon family used in early IBM cloud systems.
details.benchmark: Important pre-utility cloud generation.

machines.caveats: Use live platform metadata for current backend details.

machines.name: IBM Quantum Experience 5-qubit system

details.processor: Early public cloud-accessible superconducting quantum computer.
details.benchmark: Opened hands-on cloud quantum computing to a broad audience.

machines.caveats: Historical record for origin tracking.

machines.name: Google Willow

details.processor: Superconducting chip fabricated in Google's Santa Barbara quantum facility.
details.benchmark: Reported below-threshold quantum error correction and random-circuit-sampling results.
details.software: Cirq and Google Quantum AI tooling

machines.caveats: Not a public general cloud machine record.

machines.name: Google Sycamore

details.processor: Superconducting processor used for random circuit sampling experiments.
details.benchmark: Known for the 2019 beyond-classical random circuit sampling claim.
details.software: Cirq and Google Quantum AI tooling

machines.caveats: Historical research system, not a public cloud QPU.

machines.name: Quantinuum System Model H1
machines.access: Quantinuum direct, Microsoft Azure, partner programmes

details.processor: First-generation trapped-ion system with linear architecture.
details.connectivity: All-to-all effective connectivity through ion shuttling.
details.benchmark: Quantinuum describes H1 as first commercial quantum computer to reach three-9s fidelity.
details.software: TKET, Quantinuum Nexus, Azure Quantum integrations

machines.caveats: Use provider calibration data for exact qubit count and availability.

machines.name: Quantinuum System Model H2
machines.qubits: generation-specific; often reported as a 50+ qubit class system
machines.access: Quantinuum direct, Microsoft Azure, partner programmes

details.processor: Second-generation trapped-ion system with racetrack architecture.
details.benchmark: Used in high-fidelity and error-correction demonstrations.
details.software: TKET, Quantinuum Nexus, Azure Quantum integrations

machines.caveats: Exact active configuration should be fetched from the provider or cloud access plan.

machines.name: Quantinuum Helios

details.processor: Latest-generation Quantinuum trapped-ion system line.
details.connectivity: All-to-all effective connectivity with mid-circuit measurement and qubit reuse.
details.benchmark: Provider positions it for large-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computing progress.

machines.caveats: Keep as a family record unless a precise installed Helios machine is indexed.

machines.name: IonQ Aria

details.processor: Trapped-ion system with high connectivity.
details.benchmark: Part of IonQ's commercial trapped-ion platform history.
details.software: IonQ API, Qiskit provider, Cirq integrations, partner cloud SDKs

machines.caveats: Availability and calibration are cloud-dependent.

machines.name: IonQ Forte-1

details.processor: Commercial trapped-ion QPU.
details.benchmark: Published as a Braket QPU device.

machines.caveats: Use Braket GetDevice or IonQ status for live properties.

machines.name: IonQ Forte Enterprise-1
machines.access: Amazon Braket and IonQ enterprise channels

details.processor: Enterprise trapped-ion system generation.
details.benchmark: Listed as a gate-based QPU in Amazon Braket supported devices.

machines.caveats: AQ and qubit records should be source-stamped when refreshed.

machines.name: IonQ Tempo
machines.qubits: roadmap / AQ64 class claim; verify current source before operational use

details.processor: Next-generation IonQ trapped-ion system line.
details.benchmark: IonQ roadmap target/claim record.
details.software: IonQ API and partner SDKs where exposed

machines.caveats: Treat as a roadmap record unless backed by live QPU metadata.

machines.name: D-Wave Advantage2
machines.qubits: thousands of annealing qubits; check system generation
machines.access: D-Wave Leap cloud or on-premises deployment

details.processor: Production annealing quantum system for optimization.
details.benchmark: Provider positions Advantage2 as available through Leap or on-premises.

machines.caveats: Annealing systems are not gate-model QPUs; compare by workload, not raw qubit count.

machines.name: D-Wave Advantage
machines.access: D-Wave Leap cloud or customer deployments

details.processor: Annealing quantum computer built for optimization problems.
details.benchmark: Commercial annealing generation before Advantage2.

machines.caveats: Not gate-model; current availability should be checked in Leap.

machines.name: QuEra Aquila

details.processor: Field-programmable neutral-atom array for analog Hamiltonian simulation.
details.benchmark: Listed by Amazon Braket as a QuEra QPU.

machines.caveats: Analog machine, not a universal gate-model backend.

machines.name: Rigetti Ankaa-3

details.processor: Superconducting QPU generation from Rigetti.
details.benchmark: Listed by Amazon Braket as a gate-based QPU.
details.software: Amazon Braket SDK, pyQuil / Quil ecosystem

machines.caveats: Use live device properties for topology and calibration.

machines.name: Rigetti Cepheus-1-108Q

details.processor: Superconducting QPU listed in Amazon Braket supported devices.
details.connectivity: Rigetti topology; retrieve live device properties for edges.
details.benchmark: Cloud-listed gate-based QPU.
details.software: Amazon Braket SDK, pyQuil / Quil ecosystem

machines.caveats: Check live access plan and calibration before scheduling jobs.

machines.name: Rigetti Aspen-M

details.processor: Multi-chip superconducting QPU generation.
details.benchmark: Important prior Rigetti scale milestone.

machines.caveats: May not be an active public backend.

machines.name: IQM Garnet

details.processor: 20-qubit superconducting QPU.
details.connectivity: IQM superconducting topology; retrieve live device properties.
details.benchmark: Braket-listed QPU; IQM has published benchmarks for the Garnet QPU.
details.software: Amazon Braket SDK, IQM SDK integrations

machines.caveats: Live calibration changes over time.

machines.name: IQM Emerald

details.processor: Superconducting QPU listed by Amazon Braket.
details.connectivity: IQM topology; retrieve live device properties.
details.benchmark: Cloud-listed gate-based QPU.
details.software: Amazon Braket SDK, IQM SDK integrations

machines.caveats: Use Braket device properties for exact topology.

machines.name: AQT IBEX-Q1

details.processor: Trapped-ion QPU listed by Amazon Braket.
details.benchmark: Cloud-listed gate-based QPU.

machines.caveats: Exact properties must be pulled from live Braket metadata.

machines.name: OQC Lucy

details.processor: OQC coaxmon superconducting QPU generation.
details.benchmark: Early European commercial cloud-accessible QPU.

machines.caveats: Not necessarily active on current public clouds.

machines.name: OQC Toshiko

details.processor: OQC enterprise superconducting QPU generation.
details.benchmark: Commercial European system family.

machines.caveats: Verify current access and calibration with OQC.

machines.name: Xanadu Borealis
machines.qubits: photonic modes rather than qubits; 216 squeezed modes reported historically

details.processor: Programmable photonic processor for Gaussian boson sampling.
details.benchmark: Known for quantum computational advantage demonstrations.

machines.caveats: Not directly comparable to gate-model qubit counts.

machines.name: PASQAL Fresnel

details.processor: Neutral-atom quantum processor family.
details.benchmark: Commercial neutral-atom system family.

machines.caveats: Index exact installed machines separately when known.

machines.name: Atom Computing Phoenix

details.processor: Neutral-atom array processor family.
details.benchmark: Known for public 1000+ atom scale milestone reporting.

machines.caveats: Treat as a public milestone until live service metadata is available.

machines.name: Intel Tunnel Falls

details.processor: Silicon spin-qubit research chip fabricated with semiconductor manufacturing methods.
details.benchmark: Used to explore manufacturable silicon spin-qubit scaling.

machines.caveats: Research chip rather than public QPU service.

machines.name: Microsoft Majorana 1

details.processor: Topological quantum chip announced by Microsoft.
details.benchmark: Claims and interpretation remain scientifically debated.
details.software: Azure Quantum ecosystem when future systems are exposed

machines.caveats: Record as an announced research chip; do not treat as a proven operational public QPU.

machines.name: AWS Ocelot

details.processor: AWS research chip exploring cat-qubit error correction.
details.benchmark: Research prototype, distinct from Braket partner QPUs.
details.software: Amazon Braket ecosystem for cloud quantum access

machines.caveats: Do not confuse with Braket-listed commercial QPUs.

machines.name: USTC Jiuzhang
machines.provider: University of Science and Technology of China
machines.qubits: photonic modes / detected photons, not gate-model qubits

details.processor: Photonic sampling experiment.
details.benchmark: Known for quantum advantage claims in boson sampling.

machines.caveats: Not a programmable cloud gate-model computer.

machines.name: USTC Zuchongzhi
machines.provider: University of Science and Technology of China

details.processor: Superconducting research processor used in random circuit sampling work.
details.benchmark: Known for quantum advantage experiments.

machines.caveats: Not a public cloud QPU record.

machines.name: Honeywell System Model H0

details.processor: Pre-Quantinuum trapped-ion system generation.
details.benchmark: Historical predecessor to Quantinuum H-series.

machines.caveats: Historical system, superseded by H-series branding.

machines.name: D-Wave One
machines.access: sold as early commercial quantum annealing system

details.processor: Early commercial quantum annealing computer.
details.benchmark: Important commercial quantum-computing history point.

machines.caveats: Historical system, not comparable to gate-model QPUs by raw qubits.

machines.name: IBM Quantum System One
machines.country: United States / Germany / Japan / Canada / Spain / South Korea
machines.site: multiple installed IBM Quantum System One sites
machines.access: dedicated installations and IBM Quantum services

details.processor: Integrated cryogenic, control, and superconducting processor system.
details.benchmark: First IBM integrated commercial quantum computer product line.
details.software: Qiskit Runtime and IBM Quantum Platform

machines.caveats: Processor generation and backend availability vary by installation.

machines.name: Google Bristlecone

details.processor: Superconducting processor announced before Sycamore.
details.benchmark: Research stepping stone toward larger random-circuit-sampling experiments.
details.software: Cirq and Google Quantum AI tooling

machines.caveats: Historical research chip, not a public cloud QPU.

machines.name: IonQ Harmony
machines.access: historical cloud access through partner platforms

details.processor: Early IonQ trapped-ion cloud QPU preceding Aria and Forte.
details.benchmark: Important early commercial trapped-ion cloud system.
details.software: IonQ API, Amazon Braket, Azure Quantum where historically exposed

machines.caveats: Use current IonQ platform metadata for live availability.

machines.name: D-Wave 2000Q
machines.access: D-Wave Leap historical generation and customer installations

details.processor: Commercial quantum annealing system generation before Advantage.
details.benchmark: Used widely in early annealing application studies.

machines.caveats: Annealing qubit counts are not comparable with gate-model logical qubits.

machines.name: Origin Quantum Wukong
machines.qubits: 72 physical class as publicly reported for Wukong

details.processor: Chinese superconducting cloud-accessible quantum computer.
details.benchmark: Public-facing Chinese quantum cloud milestone.

machines.caveats: Provider-specific calibration and access status should be checked live.

machines.name: RIKEN 64-qubit superconducting quantum computer

details.processor: National superconducting quantum computer launched by RIKEN and partners.
details.benchmark: Japanese domestic quantum-computing milestone.

machines.caveats: Access rules and backend names are programme-specific.

machines.name: Fujitsu / RIKEN 64-qubit superconducting quantum computer
machines.access: joint research and cloud access programme

details.processor: Jointly developed superconducting quantum computer connected to a hybrid computing platform.
details.benchmark: Part of Japanese hybrid quantum-classical computing services.

machines.caveats: Record separately from the broader RIKEN launch because access and platform ownership differ by programme.

machines.name: Quandela Ascella
machines.qubits: photonic qubit count depends on exposed processor generation

details.processor: Photonic quantum processing unit based on single-photon technology.
details.benchmark: European photonic cloud QPU offering.

machines.caveats: Photonic resource metrics differ from superconducting and ion-trap qubit counts.

machines.name: ORCA PT Series
machines.qubits: photonic modes / time-bin resources, not gate-model qubits

details.processor: Room-temperature photonic quantum computing system family.
details.connectivity: Photonic memory and optical network architecture.
details.benchmark: Commercial photonic quantum accelerator line.

machines.caveats: Resource metrics are not directly comparable with gate-model physical qubits.

machines.name: Quantum Computing Inc. Dirac-3

details.processor: Photonic entropy computing system positioned for optimization workloads.
details.benchmark: Application-oriented optimization system, not a universal gate-model QPU.

machines.caveats: Classify separately from universal gate-model machines.

machines.name: SpinQ Gemini

details.processor: Desktop NMR quantum computer for education and demonstrations.
details.benchmark: Useful as an accessible physical quantum computer rather than a scalable research machine.

machines.caveats: Educational NMR system; not comparable with cloud-scale research QPUs.

machines.name: Alice & Bob Boson

details.processor: Cat-qubit superconducting hardware programme focused on error suppression.
details.benchmark: Fault-tolerance roadmap hardware rather than a broad public cloud backend.

machines.caveats: Track as a research/pre-commercial family until public backend details stabilize.
